fields.description:
## Why
Bin-packing contamination is UNMEASURABLE: the sidecar stamps each finding's criteria but throws away chunk co-residency (only a chunk COUNT survives). Cannot calibrate R-1-part-2 (epic fort-wisp-wren) without measuring it.

## What (verified code targets)
- Stamp each Pass-1 finding with its chunk COHORT (sorted criterion-id set). Findings come from THREE finder paths: pass1_chunk (passes.py def@255) -- multi-criterion chunk, cohort = the chunk's sorted ids; pass1_container (def@310) -- cohort = its container criteria ids; pass1_isf (def@409) -- runs as a SINGLE fixed call (not a multi-criterion chunk), so cohort = a singleton ["ISF"] (define this explicitly; ISF is never co-resident, so its contamination cohort is itself).
- Carry the key through _slim (sidecar.py def@228). CORRECTION: _slim uses an explicit key-ALLOWLIST return dict and deliberately excludes runtime-only carriers -- cohort must be ADDED to that allowlist or it is stripped. cohort is a small scalar list, safe to persist.
- Old sidecars / pre-change findings lack cohort: offline analysis must treat a MISSING cohort as "unknown" (skip from the contamination rate), not empty. State this fallback.
- Out-of-plan_review callers of the finding shape: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py (~line 100) and src/rebar/llm/workflow/criterion_preview.py (~line 365) call pass1_chunk -- confirm they tolerate the extra key.
- Document the cohort field in a sidecar schema note (docs).

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] cohort (sorted chunk criterion-id set; ISF singleton ["ISF"]) stamped on findings from pass1_chunk AND pass1_container AND pass1_isf, and added to _slim's allowlist. Proof: run a minimal review; `jq '.findings[].cohort' <sidecar>` non-null for all three finder types; author cases in tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py (the ACTUAL sidecar test file) asserting cohort content correctness; `pytest tests/unit/test_sidecar_enrichment.py -k cohort` green.
- [ ] Sidecar schema note added for cohort; offline analysis treats missing cohort as unknown (documented).
- [ ] Both out-of-tree callers (eval_solver.py, criterion_preview.py) still pass. Proof: their existing tests green.
- [ ] A small script/query computes a blocking-tier contamination rate from cohort data (committed under scripts/ or a test). Unblocks fort-wisp-wren.
